name: ednet

env_axis.name: subset
env_axis.shift_mechanism: subset progression KT1→KT4 expands action-type richness; potentially also behavior shift

facts: 131,441,538 interactions from 784,309 students across KT1/KT2/KT3/KT4 subsets (TOEIC-prep platform Santa, 2017-19).
hypotheses: KT1→KT4 is a schema-expansion shift (more action types tracked) rather than a behavior shift, because the progression primarily adds instrumentation rather than changing student conduct.
Knowledge-tracing models trained on KT1 transfer to KT4 only partially and require re-training for the richer action set.
intuitions: Knowledge-tracing benchmark surveys from 2024-25 likely cover EdNet.
Foundation education-model attempts using EdNet probably exist.
unknowns: Whether learning-process generalization here transfers to other domains cannot be decided — EdNet is TOEIC-prep-specific (Korean students, English assessment).
The Santa platform UI shapes student behavior, but UI-as-confounder is not exposed in the data.
epistemic_state.boundary: Single-platform / single-subject (TOEIC) — cannot evidence cross-domain learning shift.

representational_commitments: KT1-KT4 progression is curator's nested scheme
materialization_status: All 5 subsets via Riiid's official bit.ly/ednet_kt* Google Drive links (corrected file IDs from earlier guessed ones): KT1 1.2GB, KT2 555MB, KT3 762MB, KT4 1.2GB, Contents 0.6MB. Total 3.5 GB compressed.
